deep_dive.title: Dustin: Draft-Augmented Sparse Verification for Efficient Long-Context Generation with Speculative Decoding
deep_dive.abstract: While speculative decoding improves inference throughput for multi-batch long-context Large Language Models (LLMs), its efficiency is often limited by a verification bottleneck where Key-Value (KV) cache loading dominates latency. Existing compression methods fail in this regime: static eviction incurs accuracy loss due to saliency shift, while dynamic selection introduces prohibitive computational overhead during the verification path. We propose Dustin, a sparse verification framework designed for long-context speculative decoding. Dustin integrates lookahead signals from the draft model with historical attention from the target model to identify critical tokens with high fidelity across multi-step verification windows. To reduce recomputation latency, this approach further employs a sparse estimation scheme that restricts importance scoring to a minimal subset of attention heads. Evaluations on PG-19 and LongBench with Qwen2.5-72B demonstrate that Dustin achieves a 27.85x speedup in self-attention and a 9.17x end-to-end decoding speedup at a 32k sequence length, all with negligible accuracy degradation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: sparse verification for speculative decoding with draft model lookahead
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于草稿模型前瞻的稀疏验证投机解码方法
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于草稿模型前瞻的稀疏验证投机解码方法
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于草稿模型前瞻的稀疏验证投机解码方法
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Dustin proposes a sparse verification framework for long-context speculative decoding that integrates draft model lookahead signals to reduce KV cache loading overhead.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 长上下文投机解码中验证瓶颈限制了吞吐量，因为KV缓存加载成为延迟主要来源。Dustin利用草稿模型的前瞻信号与目标模型历史注意力结合，高保真地识别关键令牌用于稀疏验证。实验表明该方法在保持精度的同时显著减少KV缓存加载开销，提升了多批长上下文场景的推理效率。该方法为投机解码的验证阶段提供了一种高效的稀疏化方案。

deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 基于宽松验证的大预言模型投机解码方法, especially work that connects or combines: speculative decoding for large language models; relaxed verification mechanism in speculative decoding; approximate matching for speculative execution; relaxed acceptance criteria for token generation; lossy verification in LLM inference acceleration; accelerating LLM inference with speculative decoding; speculative decoding methods with relaxed verification for LLMs; approximate token matching in speculative decoding frameworks; improving speculative decoding efficiency via relaxed acceptance.

deep_dive.title: Forget Without Compromise: Nexus Sampling for Streaming KV-Cache Eviction Under Fixed Budgets
deep_dive.abstract: Long-context and agentic LLM workloads push the KV cache past any fixed memory budget, forcing the inference stack to permanently evict tokens at every step of a continuous-inference stream. Existing methods all share the same template, a per-step direct-attention score followed by deterministic top-$K$ selection, which converts a single below-cutoff step into an irreversible verdict and permanently erases any subtly important token that direct attention cannot single out from noise. To address this challenge, we propose Nexus Sampling, a training-free eviction method that pairs Nexus scoring, an iterative walk over direct attention that surfaces bridge tokens, with weighted reservoir sampling, which retains tokens with inclusion probability in place of deterministic top-$K$. Theoretically, we show that Nexus Sampling dominates deterministic top-$K$ in long-run survival of subtly important tokens. Empirically, at 80% KV cache eviction, Nexus Sampling matches dense attention within 1% on LongBench while outperforming top-$K$ baselines on retrieval-heavy tasks, with up to 10x smaller per-sequence cache memory.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: KV cache eviction with sampling for efficient LLM inference
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 针对LLM推理的KV缓存淘汰采样方法
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 针对LLM推理的KV缓存淘汰采样方法
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 针对LLM推理的KV缓存淘汰采样方法
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Nexus Sampling proposes a training-free eviction method for streaming KV cache under fixed budgets, using iterative scoring and weighted reservoir sampling to retain important tokens.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 长上下文和代理LLM工作负载将KV缓存推至固定内存预算之外，现有确定性淘汰方法无法恢复被误删的token。本文提出Nexus Sampling，一种无需训练的方法，通过迭代注意力评分（Nexus Scoring）识别桥接token，并结合加权蓄水池采样保留重要token。实验表明该方法在固定预算下显著优于现有淘汰策略，为LLM高效推理提供了新的缓存管理方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 长上下文和代理LLM工作负载将KV缓存推至固定内存预算之外，现有确定性淘汰方法无法恢复被误删的token。本文提出Nexus Sampling，一种无需训练的方法，通过迭代注意力评分（Nexus Scoring）识别桥接token，并结合加权蓄水池采样保留重要token。实验表明该方法在固定预算下显著优于现有淘汰策略，为LLM高效推理提供了新的缓存管理方案。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 大语言模型高效推理相关的论文检索, especially work that connects or combines: efficient large language model inference techniques; KV cache optimization and management for LLM; speculative decoding for faster LLM inference; structured and unstructured pruning for LLMs; efficient attention mechanisms like FlashAttention or PagedAttention; survey on efficient inference of large language models; state of the art techniques for LLM inference acceleration; optimizing throughput and latency for LLM serving.

deep_dive.title: GeMoE: Gating Entropy is All You Need for Uncertainty-aware Adaptive Routing in MoE-based Large Vision-Language Models
deep_dive.abstract: With the increase in model parameters and training data, the instruction following and generalization capabilities of Large VisionLanguage Models (LVLMs) have been significantly improved. Based on the Mixture of Experts (MoE) architecture, LVLMs expand their parameter capacity while maintaining the inference cost. However, traditional MoE methods employ a Top-k static routing strategy, which fails to account for variations in the input and adaptively select the number of experts, resulting in suboptimal resource utilization. In this paper, we propose viewing token routing as an information encoding task, framing dynamic routing as a Minimum Description Length (MDL) problem in encoding By validating the connection between MDL and gating entropy in the MoE scenario, we introduce Gating Entropy-based Uncertainty-aware Adaptive Routing (GeMoE) for MoE. Unlike traditional static or heuristic-based dynamic routing methods, GeMoE explicitly models the trade-off between model complexity and performance. By using gating entropy to assess the complexity of tokens, GeMoE adaptively determines the number of experts each token should engage. On a wide range of backbones and benchmarks, our method achieves 99.5% average performance retention compared to the original static routing, while improving average expert activation sparsity by 36.5%.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: dynamic expert routing for MoE-based VLM inference acceleration
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于MoE的VLM动态专家路由推理加速
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于MoE的VLM动态专家路由推理加速
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于MoE的VLM动态专家路由推理加速
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes GeMoE, which frames token routing as an information encoding task and uses Minimum Description Length to dynamically select experts for MoE-based Vision-Language Models, improving inference efficiency.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对传统MoE视觉语言模型使用固定Top-k静态路由导致资源利用次优的问题，本文提出GeMoE方法，将令牌路由重新定义为信息编码任务，通过最小描述长度原则动态选择最优专家数量。该方法在保持模型性能的同时显著降低了推理成本，实现了不确定性感知的自适应路由。

quick_skim.title: Deeper is Not Always Better: Mitigating the Alignment Tax via Confident Layer Decoding
quick_skim.abstract: Autoregressive generation in large language models (LLMs) conventionally decodes from the final layer, assuming that deeper representations yield more reliable next-token predictions. We revisit this assumption by revealing a recurring Guess-Refine-Perturb dynamic: early layers form coarse guesses, intermediate layers refine reasoning-relevant semantics, and final layers can perturb these refined predictions toward generic or alignment-preferred tokens. We introduce Confident Decoding, a training-free decoding strategy that dynamically selects the most reliable near-final layer through entropy-guided conservative backward search. We further provide a theoretical formulation of layer selection as an optimal stopping problem, showing that under bounded projection noise and dominant late-stage alignment perturbation, our search rule filters perturbation while bounding the loss relative to the oracle refinement layer. Experiments across dense and Mixture-of-Experts LLMs demonstrate consistent gains on challenging reasoning benchmarks, including GPQA-Diamond, Omni-MATH, and HLE, with zero memory overhead and less than 2% latency increase. These results suggest dynamically bypassing final-layer perturbations can unlock stronger reasoning behavior from aligned LLMs.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: training-free decoding strategy for LLM inference efficiency
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 无训练解码策略提升LLM推理效率
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 无训练解码策略提升LLM推理效率
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 无训练解码策略提升LLM推理效率
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Confident Decoding, a training-free layer selection strategy that dynamically chooses the most reliable layer for token prediction, improving LLM inference efficiency without additional training.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对自回归大语言模型固定使用最后一层解码可能带来对齐惩罚的问题，本文提出自信解码方法，通过熵引导的保守反向搜索动态选择最可靠的近最终层进行预测。该方法无需训练，在多项任务上提升了生成质量并减少了对齐偏差，为高效LLM推理提供了新的解码策略。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对自回归大语言模型固定使用最后一层解码可能带来对齐惩罚的问题，本文提出自信解码方法，通过熵引导的保守反向搜索动态选择最可靠的近最终层进行预测。该方法无需训练，在多项任务上提升了生成质量并减少了对齐偏差，为高效LLM推理提供了新的解码策略。

quick_skim.title: Look Before You Zoom: Adaptive Routing for the Resolution-Context Trade-off in Visual RAG
quick_skim.abstract: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) struggle as query-relevant objects become smaller. To address this, recent training-free approaches dynamically retrieve and zoom into local image regions. However, we show that indiscriminately applying retrieval ignores a critical vulnerability: the resolution-context trade-off. Patch-based zooming recovers details for small targets, but can split large objects and destroy global spatial context; attention-based retrieval better preserves large objects, but remains less reliable on tiny details; and global perception is often fastest when retrieval is unnecessary. Motivated by these failure modes, we introduce ViRGo (Visual Retrieval or Global Perception), a lightweight framework that formulates visual retrieval as an adaptive routing problem. ViRGo estimates object scale from the VLM's intrinsic localization heads during the initial forward pass and combines it with semantic token confidence to select between global perception, patch-based retrieval, and attention-based retrieval with minimal additional computation. Experiments across multiple VQA benchmarks and object-size groups show that ViRGo improves the accuracy-efficiency trade-off: it matches patch retrieval on small details, leverages attention-based retrieval for larger objects, and reduces inference time by routing to the global baseline when zooming is unnecessary.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: adaptive routing for VLM inference acceleration
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces ViRGo, a lightweight framework that adaptively routes between global perception and local retrieval in Visual RAG, balancing resolution and context to improve VLM inference efficiency.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对视觉语言模型在处理小目标时效果下降的问题，本文分析发现局部放大会破坏全局上下文而全局感知对小细节不敏感。为此提出ViRGo框架，根据查询特征自适应选择全局或局部处理路径，在不牺牲准确性的前提下减少不必要的检索操作，从而加速VLM推理。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对视觉语言模型在处理小目标时效果下降的问题，本文分析发现局部放大会破坏全局上下文而全局感知对小细节不敏感。为此提出ViRGo框架，根据查询特征自适应选择全局或局部处理路径，在不牺牲准确性的前提下减少不必要的检索操作，从而加速VLM推理。
